<content>1: """
2: Rollback Verification Tests for Migrations 0100-0102
3: 
4: This module tests database migration rollbacks for:
5: - 0100_add_search_vector.py - Adds search_vector field and GIN index
6: - 0101_add_context_summary.py - Adds context_summary field
7: - 0102_add_chunk_scale.py - Adds chunk_scale field
8: 
9: Test coverage:
10: 1. Individual rollback for each migration
11: 2. Complete rollback of all 3 migrations
12: 3. Row count preservation during rollbacks
13: 4. Data integrity after rollbacks
14: 5. Idempotency of rollback and reapply
15: """
16: 
17: import pytest
18: import torch
19: from django.core.management import call_command
20: from django.db import connection
21: from django.db.models import Count
22: from django.test.utils import override_settings
23: 
24: from khoj.database.models import Entry, KhojUser, SearchModelConfig
25: from tests.helpers import SearchModelFactory, UserFactory
26: 
27: 
28: # ============================================================================
29: # Fixtures
30: # ============================================================================
31: 
32: 
33: @pytest.fixture
34: def test_user(db):
35:     """Create a test user for rollback tests."""
36:     return UserFactory(username="rollback_test_user", email="rollback@test.com")
37: 
38: 
39: @pytest.fixture
40: def search_model(db):
41:     """Create a search model configuration."""
42:     return SearchModelFactory()
43: 
44: 
45: @pytest.fixture
46: def sample_embeddings():
47:     """Generate sample embeddings for testing."""
48:     return torch.randn(384).tolist()
49: 
50: 
51: @pytest.fixture
52: def migration_executor():
53:     """Get the migration executor for running migrations programmatically."""
54:     from django.db.migrations.executor import MigrationExecutor
55: 
56:     return MigrationExecutor(connection)
57: 
58: 
59: @pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
60: def ensure_initial_state(db):
61:     """Ensure database is at migration 0099 before each test."""
62:     # Start fresh at 0099
63:     call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
64:     yield
65:     # Cleanup: migrate back to latest after test
66:     call_command("migrate", "database", verbosity=0)
67: 
68: 
69: # ============================================================================
70: # Helper Functions
71: # ============================================================================
72: 
73: 
74: def get_row_count(table_name="database_entry"):
75:     """Get the row count for a given table."""
76:     with connection.cursor() as cursor:
77:         cursor.execute(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {table_name}")
78:         return cursor.fetchone()[0]
79: 
80: 
81: def column_exists(table_name, column_name):
82:     """Check if a column exists in a table."""
83:     with connection.cursor() as cursor:
84:         cursor.execute(
85:             """
86:             SELECT EXISTS (
87:                 SELECT 1 
88:                 FROM information_schema.columns 
89:                 WHERE table_name = %s AND column_name = %s
90:             )
91:             """,
92:             [table_name, column_name],
93:         )
94:         return cursor.fetchone()[0]
95: 
96: 
97: def index_exists(table_name, index_name):
98:     """Check if an index exists on a table."""
99:     with connection.cursor() as cursor:
100:         cursor.execute(
101:             """
102:             SELECT EXISTS (
103:                 SELECT 1 
104:                 FROM pg_indexes 
105:                 WHERE tablename = %s AND indexname = %s
106:             )
107:             """,
108:             [table_name, index_name],
109:         )
110:         return cursor.fetchone()[0]
111: 
112: 
113: def get_current_migration():
114:     """Get the current migration version for the database app."""
115:     with connection.cursor() as cursor:
116:         cursor.execute(
117:             """
118:             SELECT name FROM django_migrations 
119:             WHERE app = 'database' 
120:             ORDER BY applied DESC 
121:             LIMIT 1
122:             """
123:         )
124:         result = cursor.fetchone()
125:         return result[0] if result else None
126: 
127: 
128: def create_test_entry(user, search_model, sample_embeddings, **extra_fields):
129:     """Helper to create a test entry with the given fields."""
130:     return Entry.objects.create(
131:         user=user,
132:         embeddings=sample_embeddings,
133:         raw="Test raw content for rollback",
134:         compiled="Test compiled content for rollback",
135:         heading="Test Heading",
136:         file_type=Entry.EntryType.PLAINTEXT,
137:         file_path="/test/path/rollback.txt",
138:         file_name="rollback.txt",
139:         hashed_value=f"rollback_hash_{get_row_count()}",
140:         search_model=search_model,
141:         **extra_fields,
142:     )
143: 
144: 
145: # ============================================================================
146: # Test Case 1: Rollback 0102 - Removes chunk_scale
147: # ============================================================================
148: 
149: 
150: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
151: class TestRollback0102RemovesChunkScale:
152:     """Test that rollback of migration 0102 removes chunk_scale column."""
153: 
154:     def test_rollback_0102_removes_chunk_scale(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
155:         """
156:         Test: Apply migration 0102, create entries with chunk_scale, rollback to 0101,
157:         verify chunk_scale column is removed and row count is preserved.
158:         """
159:         # Step 1: Apply migration 0102
160:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
161: 
162:         # Verify we're at 0102
163:         assert "0102" in get_current_migration()
164: 
165:         # Step 2: Create entries with chunk_scale values
166:         entries = []
167:         for i in range(5):
168:             entry = create_test_entry(
169:                 test_user,
170:                 search_model,
171:                 sample_embeddings,
172:                 chunk_scale=["512", "1024", "2048", "default", "1024"][i],
173:             )
174:             entries.append(entry)
175: 
176:         # Record row count before rollback
177:         row_count_before = get_row_count()
178:         assert row_count_before == 5
179: 
180:         # Verify chunk_scale column exists
181:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
182: 
183:         # Step 3: Rollback to 0101
184:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
185: 
186:         # Step 4: Verify chunk_scale column is removed
187:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale"), (
188:             "chunk_scale column should be removed after rollback"
189:         )
190: 
191:         # Step 5: Verify row count is preserved
192:         row_count_after = get_row_count()
193:         assert row_count_after == row_count_before, (
194:             f"Row count changed after rollback: {row_count_before} -> {row_count_after}"
195:         )
196: 
197:         # Step 6: Verify entries still exist (can query without chunk_scale)
198:         remaining_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user)
199:         assert remaining_entries.count() == 5
200: 
201:     def test_rollback_0102_preserves_other_new_fields(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
202:         """
203:         Test: When rolling back 0102, ensure other fields (search_vector, context_summary) are preserved.
204:         """
205:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
206: 
207:         # Apply up to 0102
208:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
209: 
210:         # Create entry with all new fields
211:         entry = create_test_entry(
212:             test_user,
213:             search_model,
214:             sample_embeddings,
215:             chunk_scale="1024",
216:             context_summary="Test context summary",
217:         )
218: 
219:         # Set search_vector
220:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
221: 
222:         # Verify all fields exist
223:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
224:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
225:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
226: 
227:         # Rollback to 0101 (removes only chunk_scale)
228:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
229: 
230:         # Verify only chunk_scale is removed
231:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
232:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
233:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
234: 
235: 
236: # ============================================================================
237: # Test Case 2: Rollback 0101 - Removes context_summary
238: # ============================================================================
239: 
240: 
241: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
242: class TestRollback0101RemovesContextSummary:
243:     """Test that rollback of migration 0101 removes context_summary column."""
244: 
245:     def test_rollback_0101_removes_context_summary(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
246:         """
247:         Test: Apply migration 0101, create entries with context_summary, rollback to 0100,
248:         verify context_summary column is removed and row count is preserved.
249:         """
250:         # Step 1: Apply migration 0101
251:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
252: 
253:         # Verify we're at 0101
254:         assert "0101" in get_current_migration()
255: 
256:         # Step 2: Create entries with context_summary
257:         summaries = ["Summary 1", "Summary 2", "Summary 3", None, "Summary 5"]
258:         for i, summary in enumerate(summaries):
259:             create_test_entry(
260:                 test_user,
261:                 search_model,
262:                 sample_embeddings,
263:                 context_summary=summary,
264:             )
265: 
266:         # Record row count before rollback
267:         row_count_before = get_row_count()
268:         assert row_count_before == 5
269: 
270:         # Verify context_summary column exists
271:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
272: 
273:         # Step 3: Rollback to 0100
274:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
275: 
276:         # Step 4: Verify context_summary column is removed
277:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary"), (
278:             "context_summary column should be removed after rollback"
279:         )
280: 
281:         # Step 5: Verify row count is preserved
282:         row_count_after = get_row_count()
283:         assert row_count_after == row_count_before, (
284:             f"Row count changed after rollback: {row_count_before} -> {row_count_after}"
285:         )
286: 
287:     def test_rollback_0101_preserves_search_vector(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
288:         """
289:         Test: When rolling back 0101, ensure search_vector field is preserved.
290:         """
291:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
292: 
293:         # Apply up to 0101
294:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
295: 
296:         # Create entry with context_summary
297:         entry = create_test_entry(
298:             test_user,
299:             search_model,
300:             sample_embeddings,
301:             context_summary="Test summary",
302:         )
303: 
304:         # Set search_vector (simulating 0100 is applied)
305:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
306: 
307:         # Verify both fields exist
308:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
309:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
310: 
311:         # Rollback to 0100 (removes only context_summary)
312:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
313: 
314:         # Verify only context_summary is removed
315:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
316:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
317:

319: # ============================================================================
320: # Test Case 3: Rollback 0100 - Removes search_vector and GIN index
321: # ============================================================================
322: 
323: 
324: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
325: class TestRollback0100RemovesSearchVector:
326:     """Test that rollback of migration 0100 removes search_vector column and GIN index."""
327: 
328:     def test_rollback_0100_removes_search_vector(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
329:         """
330:         Test: Apply migration 0100, create entries with search_vector, rollback to 0099,
331:         verify search_vector column and GIN index are removed, row count preserved.
332:         """
333:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
334: 
335:         # Step 1: Apply migration 0100
336:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
337: 
338:         # Verify we're at 0100
339:         assert "0100" in get_current_migration()
340: 
341:         # Step 2: Create entries with search_vector
342:         for i in range(10):
343:             entry = create_test_entry(test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings)
344:             # Set search_vector
345:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw", "compiled"))
346: 
347:         # Record row count before rollback
348:         row_count_before = get_row_count()
349:         assert row_count_before == 10
350: 
351:         # Verify search_vector column and GIN index exist
352:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
353:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
354: 
355:         # Step 3: Rollback to 0099
356:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
357: 
358:         # Step 4: Verify search_vector column is removed
359:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector"), (
360:             "search_vector column should be removed after rollback"
361:         )
362: 
363:         # Step 5: Verify GIN index is removed
364:         assert not index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx"), (
365:             "GIN index on search_vector should be removed after rollback"
366:         )
367: 
368:         # Step 6: Verify row count is preserved
369:         row_count_after = get_row_count()
370:         assert row_count_after == row_count_before, (
371:             f"Row count changed after rollback: {row_count_before} -> {row_count_after}"
372:         )
373: 
374:         # Step 7: Verify entries still exist and are queryable
375:         remaining_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user)
376:         assert remaining_entries.count() == 10
377: 
378:     def test_rollback_0100_removes_gin_index_only(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
379:         """
380:         Test: Verify that rolling back 0100 specifically removes the GIN index.
381:         """
382:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
383: 
384:         # Apply 0100
385:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
386: 
387:         # Create entry and set search_vector
388:         entry = create_test_entry(test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings)
389:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
390: 
391:         # Verify GIN index exists
392:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
393: 
394:         # Rollback
395:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
396: 
397:         # Verify GIN index is removed
398:         assert not index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
399: 
400: 
401: # ============================================================================
402: # Test Case 4: Complete Rollback - All 3 Migrations
403: # ============================================================================
404: 
405: 
406: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
407: class TestCompleteRollbackAllMigrations:
408:     """Test complete rollback of all 3 migrations (0100, 0101, 0102) to 0099."""
409: 
410:     def test_complete_rollback_all_migrations(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
411:         """
412:         Test: Apply all 3 migrations, create 100 test entries, rollback all,
413:         verify all columns removed and row count unchanged.
414:         """
415:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
416: 
417:         # Step 1: Apply all 3 migrations
418:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
419: 
420:         # Verify we're at 0102
421:         assert "0102" in get_current_migration()
422: 
423:         # Step 2: Create 100 test entries with all new fields
424:         scales = ["512", "1024", "2048", "default"]
425:         for i in range(100):
426:             entry = create_test_entry(
427:                 test_user,
428:                 search_model,
429:                 sample_embeddings,
430:                 chunk_scale=scales[i % 4],
431:                 context_summary=f"Context summary for entry {i}",
432:             )
433:             # Set search_vector
434:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
435: 
436:         # Step 3: Record row count
437:         row_count_before = get_row_count()
438:         assert row_count_before == 100
439: 
440:         # Verify all columns and index exist
441:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
442:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
443:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
444:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
445: 
446:         # Step 4: Rollback all 3 migrations to 0099
447:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
448: 
449:         # Step 5: Verify all columns removed
450:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector"), (
451:             "search_vector should be removed after complete rollback"
452:         )
453:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary"), (
454:             "context_summary should be removed after complete rollback"
455:         )
456:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale"), (
457:             "chunk_scale should be removed after complete rollback"
458:         )
459: 
460:         # Step 6: Verify GIN index removed
461:         assert not index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx"), (
462:             "GIN index should be removed after complete rollback"
463:         )
464: 
465:         # Step 7: Verify row count unchanged
466:         row_count_after = get_row_count()
467:         assert row_count_after == row_count_before, (
468:             f"Row count changed after complete rollback: {row_count_before} -> {row_count_after}"
469:         )
470: 
471:         # Step 8: Verify entries still queryable
472:         remaining_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user)
473:         assert remaining_entries.count() == 100
474: 
475:     def test_staged_rollback_all_migrations(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
476:         """
477:         Test: Rollback migrations one at a time and verify correct columns removed at each stage.
478:         """
479:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
480: 
481:         # Apply all migrations
482:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
483: 
484:         # Create test entry with all fields
485:         entry = create_test_entry(
486:             test_user,
487:             search_model,
488:             sample_embeddings,
489:             chunk_scale="1024",
490:             context_summary="Test summary",
491:         )
492:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
493: 
494:         # Stage 1: Rollback to 0101 (removes chunk_scale only)
495:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
496:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
497:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
498:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
499:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
500: 
501:         # Stage 2: Rollback to 0100 (removes context_summary only)
502:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
503:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
504:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
505:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
506:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
507: 
508:         # Stage 3: Rollback to 0099 (removes search_vector and GIN index)
509:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
510:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
511:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
512:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
513:         assert not index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
514: 
515:         # Verify entry still exists
516:         assert Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).exists()
517: 
518: 
519: # ============================================================================
520: # Test Case 5: Rollback Preserves Existing Data
521: # ============================================================================
522: 
523: 
524: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
525: class TestRollbackPreservesExistingData:
526:     """Test that rollback preserves data that existed before migrations."""
527: 
528:     def test_rollback_preserves_original_data(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
529:         """
530:         Test: Create entries before migrations, apply then rollback migrations,
531:         verify original data is still intact.
532:         """
533:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
534: 
535:         # Step 1: Create entries at 0099 (before any new migrations)
536:         original_entries = []
537:         for i in range(10):
538:             entry = create_test_entry(
539:                 test_user,
540:                 search_model,
541:                 sample_embeddings,
542:                 raw=f"Original raw content {i}",
543:                 compiled=f"Original compiled content {i}",
544:                 hashed_value=f"original_hash_{i}",
545:             )
546:             original_entries.append(entry)
547: 
548:         # Store original data for verification
549:         original_data = {
550:             entry.id: {
551:                 "raw": entry.raw,
552:                 "compiled": entry.compiled,
553:                 "hashed_value": entry.hashed_value,
554:             }
555:             for entry in original_entries
556:         }
557: 
558:         # Step 2: Apply all migrations
559:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
560: 
561:         # Step 3: Update entries with new fields
562:         for i, entry in enumerate(original_entries):
563:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(
564:                 chunk_scale="1024",
565:                 context_summary=f"Added summary {i}",
566:                 search_vector=SearchVector("raw"),
567:             )
568: 
569:         # Step 4: Rollback all migrations
570:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
571: 
572:         # Step 5: Verify original data is preserved
573:         for entry_id, original in original_data.items():
574:             entry = Entry.objects.get(id=entry_id)
575:             assert entry.raw == original["raw"], (
576:                 f"Raw content changed for entry {entry_id}"
577:             )
578:             assert entry.compiled == original["compiled"], (
579:                 f"Compiled content changed for entry {entry_id}"
580:             )
581:             assert entry.hashed_value == original["hashed_value"], (
582:                 f"Hashed value changed for entry {entry_id}"
583:             )
584: 
585:     def test_rollback_preserves_row_count_with_existing_data(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
586:         """
587:         Test: Mix of existing and new entries, verify all preserved after rollback.
588:         """
589:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
590: 
591:         # Create initial entries
592:         initial_count = 25
593:         for i in range(initial_count):
594:             create_test_entry(
595:                 test_user,
596:                 search_model,
597:                 sample_embeddings,
598:                 raw=f"Initial entry {i}",
599:                 hashed_value=f"initial_hash_{i}",
600:             )
601: 
602:         # Record count
603:         count_before_migrations = get_row_count()
604:         assert count_before_migrations == initial_count
605: 
606:         # Apply migrations
607:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
608: 
609:         # Create more entries with new fields
610:         additional_count = 15
611:         for i in range(additional_count):
612:             entry = create_test_entry(
613:                 test_user,
614:                 search_model,
615:                 sample_embeddings,
616:                 raw=f"New entry {i}",
617:                 hashed_value=f"new_hash_{i}",
618:                 chunk_scale="512",
619:                 context_summary=f"Summary {i}",
620:             )
621:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
622: 
623:         # Verify total count
624:         count_with_new = get_row_count()
625:         assert count_with_new == initial_count + additional_count
626: 
627:         # Rollback
628:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
629: 
630:         # Verify all entries preserved
631:         count_after_rollback = get_row_count()
632:         assert count_after_rollback == count_with_new, (
633:             f"Row count mismatch after rollback: {count_with_new} -> {count_after_rollback}"
634:         )
635: 
636:         # Verify all entries queryable
637:         all_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user)
638:         assert all_entries.count() == initial_count + additional_count
639: 
640: 
641: # ============================================================================
642: # Test Case 6: Rollback and Reapply Idempotent
643: # ============================================================================
644: 
645: 
646: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
647: class TestRollbackAndReapplyIdempotent:
648:     """Test that rollback and reapply cycle is idempotent."""
649: 
650:     def test_rollback_and_reapply_idempotent(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
651:         """
652:         Test: Apply migrations, rollback, re-apply migrations, verify system works correctly.
653:         """
654:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
655: 
656:         # Step 1: Apply migrations
657:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
658: 
659:         # Step 2: Create entries with all new fields
660:         entries = []
661:         for i in range(5):
662:             entry = create_test_entry(
663:                 test_user,
664:                 search_model,
665:                 sample_embeddings,
666:                 chunk_scale="1024",
667:                 context_summary=f"Summary {i}",
668:             )
669:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
670:             entries.append(entry)
671: 
672:         # Step 3: Verify columns exist
673:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
674:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
675:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
676:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
677: 
678:         # Step 4: Rollback all migrations
679:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
680: 
681:         # Verify columns removed
682:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
683:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
684:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
685: 
686:         # Step 5: Re-apply migrations
687:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
688: 
689:         # Step 6: Verify columns restored
690:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
691:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
692:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
693:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
694: 
695:         # Step 7: Verify system works - can create new entries with all fields
696:         new_entries = []
697:         for i in range(3):
698:             entry = create_test_entry(
699:                 test_user,
700:                 search_model,
701:                 sample_embeddings,
702:                 raw=f"Reapplied entry {i}",
703:                 compiled=f"Reapplied compiled {i}",
704:                 hashed_value=f"reapplied_hash_{i}",
705:                 chunk_scale="2048",
706:                 context_summary=f"Reapplied summary {i}",
707:             )
708:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw", "compiled"))
709:             new_entries.append(entry)
710: 
711:         # Step 8: Verify all entries (old and new) exist
712:         all_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user)
713:         assert all_entries.count() == len(entries) + len(new_entries)
714: 
715:         # Step 9: Verify new entries have correct data
716:         for i, entry in enumerate(new_entries):
717:             entry.refresh_from_db()
718:             assert entry.chunk_scale == "2048"
719:             assert entry.context_summary == f"Reapplied summary {i}"
720:             assert entry.search_vector is not None
721: 
722:     def test_multiple_rollback_reapply_cycles(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
723:         """
724:         Test: Multiple rollback and reapply cycles remain idempotent.
725:         """
726:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
727: 
728:         # Perform multiple cycles
729:         for cycle in range(3):
730:             # Apply
731:             call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
732: 
733:             # Create entry
734:             entry = create_test_entry(
735:                 test_user,
736:                 search_model,
737:                 sample_embeddings,
738:                 raw=f"Cycle {cycle} entry",
739:                 chunk_scale=f"{512 * (cycle + 1)}",
740:                 context_summary=f"Cycle {cycle} summary",
741:             )
742:             Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
743: 
744:             # Verify
745:             entry.refresh_from_db()
746:             assert entry.chunk_scale == f"{512 * (cycle + 1)}"
747:             assert entry.context_summary == f"Cycle {cycle} summary"
748: 
749:             # Rollback
750:             call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
751: 
752:             # Verify entry still exists (core data preserved)
753:             assert Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).exists()
754: 
755:         # Final apply
756:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
757: 
758:         # Verify final state
759:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
760:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
761:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
762:         assert index_exists("database_entry", "entry_search_vector_gin_idx")
763: 
764:         # Verify all entries exist
765:         total_entries = Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user).count()
766:         assert total_entries == 3  # One per cycle
767: 
768: 
769: # ============================================================================
770: # Additional Edge Case Tests
771: # ============================================================================
772: 
773: 
774: @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)
775: class TestRollbackEdgeCases:
776:     """Test edge cases for migration rollbacks."""
777: 
778:     def test_rollback_with_no_entries(self):
779:         """
780:         Test: Rollback migrations when no entries exist.
781:         """
782:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
783: 
784:         # Apply migrations
785:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
786: 
787:         # Verify columns exist
788:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
789:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
790:         assert column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
791: 
792:         # Rollback (no entries to preserve)
793:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
794: 
795:         # Verify columns removed
796:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "search_vector")
797:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "context_summary")
798:         assert not column_exists("database_entry", "chunk_scale")
799: 
800:         # Verify row count is 0
801:         assert get_row_count() == 0
802: 
803:     def test_rollback_with_unicode_data(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
804:         """
805:         Test: Rollback preserves unicode data correctly.
806:         """
807:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
808: 
809:         # Apply migrations
810:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
811: 
812:         # Create entry with unicode content
813:         unicode_summary = "Unicode: 🚀 émojis 中文 العربية 日本語"
814:         entry = create_test_entry(
815:             test_user,
816:             search_model,
817:             sample_embeddings,
818:             raw="Unicode raw content",
819:             compiled="Unicode compiled content",
820:             chunk_scale="1024",
821:             context_summary=unicode_summary,
822:         )
823:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
824: 
825:         # Rollback
826:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
827: 
828:         # Re-apply
829:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
830: 
831:         # Verify unicode content preserved
832:         entry.refresh_from_db()
833:         assert entry.raw == "Unicode raw content"
834:         assert entry.compiled == "Unicode compiled content"
835: 
836:     def test_rollback_with_large_dataset(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
837:         """
838:         Test: Rollback with a larger dataset (1000 entries).
839:         """
840:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
841: 
842:         # Apply migrations
843:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
844: 
845:         # Create 1000 entries
846:         for i in range(1000):
847:             entry = create_test_entry(
848:                 test_user,
849:                 search_model,
850:                 sample_embeddings,
851:                 raw=f"Large dataset entry {i}",
852:                 hashed_value=f"large_hash_{i}",
853:                 chunk_scale=["512", "1024", "2048", "default"][i % 4],
854:                 context_summary=f"Summary for entry {i}",
855:             )
856:             if i % 2 == 0:  # Set search_vector on half
857:                 Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
858: 
859:         # Record count
860:         count_before = get_row_count()
861:         assert count_before == 1000
862: 
863:         # Rollback
864:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
865: 
866:         # Verify count preserved
867:         count_after = get_row_count()
868:         assert count_after == count_before
869: 
870:         # Re-apply
871:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
872: 
873:         # Verify system still works
874:         entry = create_test_entry(
875:             test_user,
876:             search_model,
877:             sample_embeddings,
878:             chunk_scale="1024",
879:             context_summary="Final entry",
880:         )
881:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
882: 
883:         assert Entry.objects.filter(user=test_user).count() == 1001
884: 
885:     def test_rollback_individual_migration_preserves_others(self, test_user, search_model, sample_embeddings):
886:         """
887:         Test: Rolling back individual migration doesn't affect other migrations.
888:         """
889:         from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector
890: 
891:         # Apply all
892:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0102", verbosity=0)
893: 
894:         # Create entries with all fields
895:         entry = create_test_entry(
896:             test_user,
897:             search_model,
898:             sample_embeddings,
899:             chunk_scale="1024",
900:             context_summary="Test summary",
901:         )
902:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("raw"))
903: 
904:         # Rollback only 0102
905:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0101", verbosity=0)
906: 
907:         # Verify 0100 and 0101 fields still work
908:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(context_summary="Updated after 0102 rollback")
909:         entry.refresh_from_db()
910:         assert entry.context_summary == "Updated after 0102 rollback"
911: 
912:         # Rollback 0101
913:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0100", verbosity=0)
914: 
915:         # Verify 0100 field still works
916:         Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).update(search_vector=SearchVector("compiled"))
917:         entry.refresh_from_db()
918:         assert entry.search_vector is not None
919: 
920:         # Rollback 0100
921:         call_command("migrate", "database", "0099", verbosity=0)
922: 
923:         # Verify entry still exists
924:         assert Entry.objects.filter(id=entry.id).exists()
